up.method: HPLC substrate depletion during main growth phase 12-18 h (mean±SD, n=3): 2.67±1.17
substrate depletion 12-18 h (mean±SD, n=3): 0.71±0.28; serves as N source under oxic conditions

growth_records.cite: Riese, Cornelius N. et al. An automated oxystat fermentation regime for microoxic cultivation of Magnetospirillum gryphiswaldense. Microbial Cell Factories (2020). DOI:10.1186/s12934-020-01469-z. [PMC7654035]
growth_records.snippet: Both oxic (95% dO2) and anoxic (0% dO2) cultures showed low lactate consumption rates with 2.67 ± 1.17 and 3.12 ± 1.17 mM h−1 OD565−1 ... growth rate of 0.07 ± 0.009 h−1 (doubling time of 9.61 ± 1.3 h). The maximal OD565 under this conditio
growth_records.notes: Table 1, oxic (95% dO2) column. µ=0.07±0.009 h-1, td=9.61±1.3 h (reported, consistent with ln2/0.07=9.90 h). Rates reported per OD565 per h (biomass-normalised), not per gDW. Optional conversion to mmol/gDW/h uses gDW/OD factor cited from Heyen & Schüler (ref 32: OD565 1.4 = 0.4 g_dw/L → 0.286 g_dw/L per OD), giving qLactate≈2.67/0.286≈9.3 mmol/gDW/h and qNitrate≈2.5 mmol/gDW/h; NOT applied to primary fields because factor is from a cited paper. pH 7.0 from HEPES-buffered preculture medium (FSM); LSM buffering not restated. Batch fermentation 28 °C, 3 L bioreactor.

up.method: substrate depletion during main growth phase 10-17 h (mean±SD, n=3): 4.63±1.02 (Table 1)

growth_records.cite: Riese, Cornelius N. et al. An automated oxystat fermentation regime for microoxic cultivation of Magnetospirillum gryphiswaldense. Microbial Cell Factories (2020). DOI:10.1186/s12934-020-01469-z. [PMC7654035]
growth_records.snippet: Maximal growth among all tested conditions was observed at 1% dO2 with a growth rate of 0.15 ± 0.007 h−1 (doubling time of 4.76 ± 0.23 h) and a maximal OD565 of 1.06 ± 0.06 ... increased NO3− consumption of 1.09 ± 0.15 mM h−1 OD565−1
growth_records.notes: Table 1, microoxic (1% dO2) column. µ=0.15±0.007 h-1, td=4.76±0.23 h (reported; ln2/0.15=4.62 h consistent). Lactate consumption uses the Table 1 value 4.63±1.02 mM h-1 OD565-1 (running text Par22 mis-labels 4.76 for microoxic lactate, which is actually the doubling time; table value used). Rates biomass-normalised per OD565. Optional gDW conversion factor 0.286 g_dw/L/OD (cited ref 32) → qLactate≈16.2, qNitrate≈3.8 mmol/gDW/h; not applied to primary fields.

growth_records.medium: Large-scale medium (LSM), nitrate raised to 10 mM

up.method: substrate depletion during main growth phase 17-23 h (mean±SD, n=3): 3.12±1.17 (Table 1)
substrate depletion 17-23 h (mean±SD, n=3): 6.13±0.83; highest nitrate consumption, respiratory electron acceptor

growth_records.cite: Riese, Cornelius N. et al. An automated oxystat fermentation regime for microoxic cultivation of Magnetospirillum gryphiswaldense. Microbial Cell Factories (2020). DOI:10.1186/s12934-020-01469-z. [PMC7654035]
growth_records.snippet: In the absence of O2 (0% dO2), the growth rate was reduced to 0.13 ± 0.005 h−1 (doubling time of 5.24 ± 0.21 h) ... After 25 h, nitrate initially present at 10 mM was completely consumed (6.13 ± 0.83 mM h−1 OD565−1)
growth_records.notes: Table 1, anoxic (0% dO2) column. µ=0.13±0.005 h-1, td=5.24±0.21 h (reported; ln2/0.13=5.33 h consistent). Under denitrification nitrate consumption ~5.6x higher than oxic because nitrate also serves as terminal electron acceptor (low Yxs_nitrate 2.5 mg_dw/mmol). Rates per OD565; gDW conversion (factor 0.286, cited ref 32) → qLactate≈10.9, qNitrate≈21.4 mmol/gDW/h; not applied to primary fields.
